name: The Cistercian Route - circular from Nulles
name_original: La ruta del Císter – circular desde Nulles
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Nulles traverse picturesque rural landscapes characterized by extensive vineyards, olive groves, and hazelnut trees. The terrain features undulating roads, often flanked by traditional dry stone walls, providing a serene agricultural backdrop. This region of Tarragona offers diverse natural settings, including agricultural lands and access to more challenging climbs, making it suitable for varied road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Nulles The most popular road cycling route is The Cistercian Route - circular from Nulles, a 56.0 miles (90.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route winds through the region's millennial wine country. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santa Maria de Salomó Church – Scenic Country Road loop from Nulles, a moderate 40.7 miles (65.5 km) path. This route offers well-paved surfaces through picturesque rural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Main Square of Prades – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Nulles - Bràfim, a 65.6 miles (105.6 km) trail leading through varied agricultural lands and offering significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Nulles is defined by picturesque vineyards, olive groves, and undulating rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through agricultural areas to difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Nulles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Nulles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Nulles?
A: Nulles offers a diverse selection of over 10 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate rides through agricultural lands to challenging routes with significant climbs.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Nulles?
A: Road cycling routes around Nulles traverse picturesque rural landscapes, characterized by extensive vineyards, olive groves, and hazelnut trees. You'll often ride on undulating roads flanked by traditional dry stone walls, providing a serene agricultural backdrop. The wider Tarragona region also offers diverse natural settings, including coastal areas and forests.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Nulles?
A: Yes, Nulles is an excellent base for challenging rides. The region, part of Catalonia, is known for its density of climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Omells de na Gaia – Mazinger Z Statue loop from Nulles - Bràfim&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial elevation gains, requiring very good fitness. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10429689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Prades – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Nulles - Bràfim&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied agricultural lands with significant climbs.

Q: What are some moderate road cycling routes suitable for good fitness levels?
A: For those seeking a moderate ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Salomó Church – Scenic Country Road loop from Nulles&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This route offers well-paved surfaces through picturesque rural landscapes and is typically completed in under 3 hours.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nulles?
A: The road cycling routes in Nulles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet, well-paved country roads, the undulating terrain through vineyards, and the access to both scenic beauty and varied riding experiences.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks to visit along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. A standout attraction in Nulles is the &lt;strong&gt;Vinícola de Nulles&lt;/strong&gt;, often referred to as the 'Wine Cathedral'. This modernist winery, built in 1917, is a historical and architectural landmark that offers wine tourism experiences. You can find more information about visiting the winery here: &lt;a href="https://www.winetourism.com/experience/tasting-and-visit-to-the-wine-cathedral-at-vinicola-de-nulles-in-catalonia/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tasting and visit to the Wine Cathedral at Vinícola de Nulles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints accessible from the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Nulles offers several natural highlights. You can find natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Tree&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7208127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine of Montferri&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic views, consider routes that pass near viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4913154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Creus&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are several lakes in the vicinity, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaià Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/493217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Catllar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Nulles?
A: The region around Nulles generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery with blooming vineyards or autumn colors. Even winter can be suitable, though checking local weather conditions is always recommended.

Q: Are there any long-distance circular road cycling routes starting from Nulles?
A: Yes, Nulles is a great starting point for longer circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962374642/the-cistercian-route-circular-from-nulles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cistercian Route - circular from Nulles&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 56-mile (90 km) trail that winds through the region's millennial wine country.

Q: Can I find routes that combine cycling with visits to other towns or villages?
A: Many routes from Nulles extend to nearby towns and villages, allowing you to explore more of the Tarragona province. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10429689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Prades – Old Bridge of Montblanc loop from Nulles - Bràfim&lt;/a&gt; takes you to the historic towns of Prades and Montblanc, offering a blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that lead towards the coast from Nulles?
A: While Nulles itself is inland, the broader Tarragona region includes coastal areas. You can find routes that extend towards the coast, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31620431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre dels Moros – Cambrils Waterfront Sign loop from Nulles - Bràfim&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate ride towards the waterfront.
tips: The Monastery of Santa María de Santes Creus is a Cistercian abbey built in the 12th century. The grandeur of the monastery is due to the donations received from the nobility who wanted to be buried here.
Nice climb between the towns of Querlo and Les Pobles with little steep slopes and a distance of close to 7 kilometres.
Beautiful road that runs along the edge of the gorge. Passing through the village of Santa Perpètua de Gaià with an impressive photo.
Structure monument built in a transition stage from Romanesque to Gothic. The church, located in Santa Coloma de Queralt, also incorporates Gothic and Baroque details due to its later extensions.
